_README: DRACO-style coverage checklists per task. Each item is a binary pass/fail check evaluated by an LLM judge against the agent's report. Items should be deterministic ('does the report contain X?') not subjective ('is X well written?').
dr_shop_0001: Lists exactly 3 distinct headphone products (not duplicates).
Every listed product has a customer rating of 4.0 stars or higher.
Every listed product has a price (in USD) and the price is between $0.01 and $500.
Every listed product has a product-page URL on the One Stop Market (starts with http://localhost:7770/ or http://shopping/ etc.).
Provides a top-pick recommendation that names ONE specific product from the list.
The recommendation includes at least one concrete reason (price, rating, feature) — not just 'it is the best'.
dr_shop_0002: All 3 products are from the shampoo or conditioner category (judged by name or context).
Products are ordered by price ascending (cheapest first).
Every product has a rating of 3.0 stars or higher.
Every product has a product-page URL on the shopping site.
dr_shop_0003: Lists exactly 3 distinct complaints (not duplicates and not generic 'Other issues' placeholder).
Each complaint has a short label (about 1-6 words) and an example review snippet.
Each example snippet appears to be a real, verbatim quote from a customer review (not paraphrased / synthesized).
The complaints cover different aspects (e.g. one about sound, one about comfort, one about durability) — not 3 variants of the same issue.
Snippets are taken from low-rated reviews (rating ≤ 3 stars), not from positive reviews.
dr_shop_0004: Includes exactly 2 products in the comparison.
Both products are from the shampoo or conditioner category.
Each product reports name, price, rating, review_count, and product_url.
Explicitly computes a price-per-star (or price/rating) ratio for each product.
Names the better-value product based on the ratio with a numeric justification.
dr_shop_0005: The product's name contains the word 'backpack' (case-insensitive).
Provides a rationale that explicitly references the selection rule (highest rating, then lowest price).
